title: Eulophus peucedanoides
taxonomy: Eulophus peucedanoides is classified within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and its morphological features. The genus Eulophus is a part of the tribe Apieae and subfamily Apioideae. The exact classification of Eulophus peucedanoides is further refined through its species-specific attributes. Understanding the taxonomic hierarchy of Eulophus peucedanoides contributes to the comprehensive knowledge of plant diversity.

intro: Eulophus peucedanoides, also known as Eulophus, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Eulophus peucedanoides include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Eulophus is commonly prepared as a powder, decoction, or oil, and is used to treat various ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin conditions.
